initial_program:
% Determine the size of the input grid
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _) }.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{C : input(_, C, _) }.

% Map a cell to the opposite quadrant (reflect over the centre)
% rows
targetRow(R,Rt) :- n_rows(N), R < N/2, Rt = R + N/2.
targetRow(R,Rt) :- n_rows(N), not (R < N/2), Rt = R - N/2.

% columns
targetCol(C,Ct) :- n_cols(M), C < M/2, Ct = C + M/2.
targetCol(C,Ct) :- n_cols(M), not (C < M/2), Ct = C - M/2.

% For every nonzero input cell create a candidate colour for its mapped position
candidate(Rt, Ct, Color) :- input(R, C, Color), targetRow(R, Rt), targetCol(C, Ct).

% Produce the output: for each target cell keep the smallest colour among the candidates
output(Rt, Ct, Color) :- candidate(Rt, Ct, Color), Color = #min{ C2 : candidate(Rt, Ct, C2) }.

#show output/3.

% columns
targetCol(C,Ct) :- n_cols(M), C < M/2, Ct = C + M/2.
targetCol(C,Ct) :- n_cols(M), C >= M/2, Ct = C - M/2.

% For every nonzero input cell create a candidate colour for its mapped position
candidate(Rt, Ct, Color) :- input(R, C, Color), targetRow(R, Rt), targetCol(C, Ct).

% Produce the output: for each target cell keep the smallest colour among the candidates
output(Rt, Ct, Color) :- candidate(Rt, Ct, Color), Color = #min{ C2 : candidate(Rt, Ct, C2) }.
